title: clematis tubulosa var. davidiana
intro: Clematis tubulosa var. davidiana, also known as David's clematis, is a plant species belonging to the Clematis genus, native to China and characterized by its fragrant white flowers and woody vines. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and terpenoid content. The health benefits of David's clematis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote respiratory health, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of David's clematis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
